titles.en: The good and the bad traditions

translations.bn: And with the preceding chain of narration, Ali ibn Mahzyar reported from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Hammad ibn Uthman, who said: Ismail al-Jufi said: I heard Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Ali, peace be upon them both, say:

translations.bn: Whoever establishes a just tradition which is then followed, he shall have the like of the reward of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their rewards. And whoever establishes an unjust tradition which is then followed, he shall bear the burden of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their burdens.
translations.de: Whoever establishes a just tradition which is then followed, he shall have the like of the reward of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their rewards. And whoever establishes an unjust tradition which is then followed, he shall bear the burden of all who act upon it, without anything being diminished from their burdens.

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: وَبِالْإِسْنَادِ الْأَوَّلِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ قَالَ قَالَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ الْجُعْفِيُّ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمَا يَقُولُ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: مَنْ سَنَّ سُنَّةَ عَدْلٍ فَاتُّبِعَ كَانَ لَهُ مِثْلُ أَجْرِ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْقُصَ مِنْ أُجُورِهِمْ شَيْءٌ وَمَنْ سَنَّ سُنَّةَ جَوْرٍ فَاتُّبِعَ كَانَ عَلَيْهِ وِزْرُ مَنْ عَمِلَ بِهَا مِنْ غَيْرِ أَنْ يَنْقُصَ مِنْ أَوْزَارِهِمْ شَيْءٌ

narrators.name_en: Abu Jafar Muhammad ibn Ali (Imam al-Baqir)

key_phrases.phrase_en: blessings of Allah upon them both

bn.أَجْر: Divine reward earned through righteous deeds
bn.جَوْر: Injustice, tyranny, and deviation from the right path
bn.سُنَّة: An established practice or tradition that becomes a norm for others to follow
bn.عَدْل: Justice, fairness, and balance in conduct and judgment
bn.وِزْر: A burden of sin or moral liability for wrongdoing

seo_questions.bn: What does Islam say about the reward for establishing good practices and the punishment for introducing unjust traditions? How does Imam al-Baqir explain ongoing reward and sin for community leaders?

similar_content_hints.description: Hadith about the reward for guiding others to good deeds being equal to the doers reward
Teaching on the multiplication of good deeds and sadaqa jariya
Traditions about the responsibility of leaders for guidance or misguidance of followers

summaries.bn: Imam al-Baqir, peace be upon him, teaches that the moral consequences of introducing customs extend beyond the individual originator. In the social context of early Islam, where community norms shaped collective behavior, this hadith addresses community leaders and influencers, warning that whoever introduces a just practice receives ongoing reward equal to every followers merit, while whoever introduces an unjust practice bears ongoing sin equal to every followers wrongdoing, without any reduction in the followers own accounts.
summaries.de: Imam al-Baqir, peace be upon him, teaches that the moral consequences of introducing customs extend beyond the individual originator. text: 19 - وبالإسناد الأول عن علي بن مهزيار، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن حماد بن عثمان قال: قال إسماعيل الجعفي : سمعت أبا جعفر محمد بن علي صلوات الله عليهما يقول: من سن سنة عدل فاتبع كان له مثل أجر من عمل بها من غير أن ينقص  من أجورهم شئ، ومن سن سنة جور فاتبع كان عليه وزر من عمل بها من غير أن ينقص من أوزارهم شئ.

en.mulla-asgharali-m-m-jaffer: 19. And with the preceding chains of narration, ‘Ali ibn Mahzyar reported from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Hammad ibn Uthman, who reported that Ismail al-Jo’fi said: I heard Abu Ja’far Muhammad ibn ‘Ali, peace be upon them both say: "Whoever sets a balanced, just tradition, which is then followed by the people, he earns the reward of everyone who acts accordingly, without they losing anything from their rewards. And whoever sets an unjust tradition, which is then followed, he shall bear the burden of those who act accordingly, without any mitigation in the burdens of the followers."
